channel.title: Comments for page "Project Palisade Hub"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Project Palisade Hub"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On recommendation from many, many people, this hub page is actively being edited to make it actually useful better at conveying the scope of what we were trying to accomplish without giving away the whole plot. Any suggestions are welcome!

It only took us 2 years to fix the hub, holy shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
tldr INcredibly good shit h ere
– hide block

absolutely brilliant scope going on here!!! really sad it took me this long to get around reading all of them but easily one of the more entertaining things to come out of 2018, and definitely a concept i can go to sleep to at night for many nights to come

feels like qntm's Fine Structure in terms of the frisson it makes me feel, but a lot tighter (which isnt a high bar tbh) AND collaborated on over the span of like what two months??? which holy shit. things like this make me sad that this site is for free. imagine being this talented and getting paid in upvotes. ugh

if anything, i'm sad each introductory branch isn't more fully-explored - there's so much that can be done with grassroots-up investigations of the mystery that isn't shown, especially given how much world has been built around the Nobody/BQ/UIU-hegemony threads. i'd love to see the ways in which these unlikely heroes tap on their resources to strike back - and of course, how they end up crushed by the superior multiversal might of Mainline (or escape it?)

10/10, this should be so much more highly rated than it is now. a masterclass in scenario-building that conveys style, finesse, and affective impact. gib more pls

ed: ok decibelle is right, the blurb does NOT do this justice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ah, the canon where the Foundation is fascist. Palisade is really interesting and I really like the concept. Also, I am glad this page exists because otherwise I'd just be searching "Palisade" in search lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No point I can see to clump these together. There are some fine articles here, but they all lose some meaning (and legitimacy) being combined like this. Good entries can stand on their own, and so they should. This also puts the related 001 proposal behind a wall of nine or so other articles, which brings in readers who don't want to deal with this stuff and just want their 001. As for the existent articles, it makes them look like advertisements for the 001. I think these would all be better if untangled.

item.title: I want to like this but I cannot.
item.content:encoded:
The concepts here are both fascinating and horrifying which seems appropriate for a doomsday article but the foundation, at least to me feels extremely out of character and surprisingly inept.

+ lengthy explanation? 
- Hide whatever

I mean doesn't the foundation have access to many pocket universes that would be unaffected by this sort of phenomenon and various means to clone humans and lifeforms to sustain humans? It seemed clear to me reading this that whatever ''prime universe'' or at least planet earth in the said universe is inevitably doomed.

So I fail to understand why anyone here{in this foundation} is not focusing on some sort of evacuation of prime earth I mean if this worm thing destroys or damages or completely reconfigures that dimension wouldn't it stop after destroying its target? leaving the foundation in a position to rebuild as in something like ''recovered from the marinas trench'' especially if they could convince other G.O.Is to work with them given the dire circumstances.

It just doesn't seem very utilitarian to destroy countless dimensions to save one and I've always viewed the foundation as well-intentioned extremist utilitarians and the moral arithmetic does not add up. None of this comes across as for the greater good to me.

Of course, maybe I am a fool and misunderstood the situation. That's equally likely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This sort of formatting doesn't work for me without some kind of context or explanation as to what the series is about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see the way this hub is divided up and I think, yeah, I have no idea how these clusters of articles and tales are related, either. Good writing on quite a few of them notwithstanding.
